title: Christmas Tree Card Holder
summary: A tree-shaped card display in two sizes — angled branch ledges get wider toward the base, holding holiday cards upright on a stepped trunk profile.
description:
Both the shorter and taller versions share the same design: three pairs of angled branch-ledge boards, each pair cut at a matching 30° angle, growing wider as they step down the tree from top to bottom — the shortest pair near the top, the widest pair at the base. Each ledge pair is a wide board paired with a narrower beveled board, giving each branch a lip deep enough to hold a stack of cards upright.

**The two sizes hold genuinely different card formats**: the shorter tree tops out at a 5-1/2" ledge and won't fit portrait-format cards, while the taller tree's 7-1/4" ledge will — pick the size based on the cards actually being displayed, not just the space available. Build the trunk and base from the 1x3 and 1x2 pieces, which anchor the branch ledges and give the tree a stable footprint.

Assemble with brad nails and glue throughout — this is a small, mostly decorative build, and there's no structural load beyond a stack of cards leaning against each ledge.

cutList.note: Long point to long point, both ends cut at 30°.

steps.title: Cut the branch ledges
steps.body: Cut the three angled branch ledge and lip pairs for the size you're building, from shortest at the top to widest at the base.

steps.title: Build the trunk and base
steps.body: Assemble the trunk, trunk base, feet, and cleats into a stable stand.

steps.title: Attach the branch ledges
steps.body: Attach the three branch ledge pairs to the trunk, widest at the bottom, narrowest at the top.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply stain or paint.

unresolvedImages.alt: Christmas tree shaped holiday card holder
